title: Intensely Creamy Spanish Chicken Croquetas
description: A comforting Spanish dish featuring chicken, heavy cream, and a peppery flavor, formed into crispy croquetas.
instructions: In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken, heavy cream, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and mix until well combined.
Add the cooked onion and garlic mixture to the chicken mixture and stir until smooth.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the mixture for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
In a shallow dish, beat the eggs and set aside.
In a separate shallow dish, place the almond flour.
Using a small cookie scoop or spoon, form the chilled chicken mixture into small balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Dip each ball into the beaten eggs, coating completely, then roll in the almond flour to coat.
Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the croquetas until golden brown and crispy.
Remove the croquetas from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
